---
title: "Instagram"
platform_slug: "instagram"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    EU: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    UK: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/meta/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/meta/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/meta/ads.yml
---

Instagram's UGC transparency is assessed through the [Meta Content Library](https://transparency.meta.com/researchtools/meta-content-library/) as a single global resource applied across Brazil, the EU, and the UK, while advertising transparency draws on the [Meta Ad Library API](https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/api/) with region-specific access regimes across the same three regions. The Meta Content Library offers data access to Instagram and other Meta platforms under very strict conditions. Although it offers an API, terms and conditions prohibit structured data extraction for external analysis; data access and analysis are hence limited to a pre-approved Secure Research Environment, making it unavailable for independent research purposes. Instagram scores minimally in UGC because, unlike the API, the Meta Content Library User Interface enables approved researchers to visualize and extract structured data on user-generated content across Meta platforms, yet popularity-based follower thresholds restrict the data available. Transparency for advertising is differentiated by region: the Meta Ad Library API for EU and UK covers all ad types served over the past year, though the EU differs from the UK in that its GUI does not support structured data extraction; Brazil, however, is limited to political and issue ads, yielding partial scores on all special criteria. Across all regions, the library provides free access, unique advertiser identifiers, and ad delivery periods, but lacks engagement metrics, granular spending data, complete targeting criteria, and specific rate-limit documentation.

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("instagram")
```




## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```
